zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir George Booth Esquire於1491年出生在英國英格蘭爾鄧翰 梅西時，他的父親Sir William Booth18歲，母親Margaret Ashton18歲。他於1515年與Lady Elizabeth Boteler在英國爾鄧翰 梅西結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1531年10月25日在英國爾鄧翰 梅西享年40歲，埋葬在英國爾包頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir George Booth Esquire于1491年出生在英国英格兰爾鄧翰 梅西时，他的父亲Sir William Booth18岁，母亲Margaret Ashton18岁。他于1515年与Lady Elizabeth Boteler在英国爾鄧翰 梅西结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1531年10月25日在英国爾鄧翰 梅西享年40岁，埋葬在英国爾包頓。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir George Booth Esquire was born in 1491, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Booth, was 18 and his mother, Margaret Ashton, was 18. He married Lady Elizabeth Boteler in 1515, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 25 October 1531, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Bowdon, Cheshire, England.

Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Boteler en 1515, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 25 octobre 1531, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Bowdon, Cheshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir George Booth Esquireが1491年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで生まれた時，父Sir William Boothは18歳，母Margaret Ashtonは18歳であった。1515年にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイでLady Elizabeth Botelerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1531年10月25日にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで40歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアボウドンにて埋葬された。

ko.Old Trafford, Greater Manchester, Inglat: 영국 잉글랜드 그리터 먼체스터 드 트래퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Sir George Booth Esquire(이)가 1491년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 던함 마시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Booth(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Ashton(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1515년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 Lady Elizabeth Boteler(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1531년 10월 25일 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 보우던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir George Booth Esquire nasceu em 1491, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Booth, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Ashton, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth Boteler em 1515, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de outubro de 1531, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Bowdon, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Dunham on the Hill, Cheshire, England: дунам он т хилл, чешир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir George Booth Esquire родился в 1491 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir William Booth было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Ashton, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Boteler в 1515 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 25 октября 1531 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности боудон, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir George Booth Esquire nació en 1491, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Booth, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Margaret Ashton, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Lady Elizabeth Boteler en 1515, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 25 de octubre de 1531, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Bowdon, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
